Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand at arm's length. Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward. Thi…

Jeremy's #1 chest compound. ~30 deg low incline - hits mid/lower chest like flat pressing AND the stubborn upper chest. Elbows in an 'arrow' ~45 deg. Lower 2-3s (the eccentric is where most growth happens).
No balancing - drive safely to failure.
Machine incline, stable path.
Jeremy's top back pick - matches pulldowns for lats but far more biceps. Drive elbows down, full hang stretch.
Same pattern if pull-ups are too hard.
Underhand - even more biceps.
Beats DB front raises by ~41% for front delt - makes front-raise work redundant. Brace glutes, press overhead, no leg drive.
Seated and strict, back supported.
Stable path to failure.
Jeremy's top mid-trap/rhomboid pick. Pin chest to an incline bench - zero lower-back sway = true upper-back failure. Drive elbows back, squeeze.
Same chest-supported pattern.
Chest-supported plate-loaded row.
Jeremy's #1 biceps move. Elbows pinned behind the body fully stretch the long head -> superior stretch-mediated growth. Lower 2-3s (the eccentric is where most growth happens).
Both arms together, same deep stretch.
Bench-supported, no swing.
Heels slightly elevated biases the quads. Brace, sit between hips, drive up. Lower 2-3s (the eccentric is where most growth happens).
Deep ATG for full quad ROM.
Box squat for consistent depth.
Hip-hinge - push hips back, soft knees, bar close, feel the hamstring stretch, stand tall.
DB version, same hinge.
Straighter legs, big hamstring stretch.
Straight-leg variation. Full stretch at the bottom, big squeeze on top, slow.
Loaded, full ROM.
Targets the soleus (bent knee).
Abdominal stabilization. Low back flat to the floor, extend opposite arm/leg slowly.
Brace, straight line, squeeze glutes.
Loaded ab flexion, crunch ribs to hips.
Great for safely driving to failure. Bar to upper chest, ~30 deg. Think: push biceps inward, not hands.
Machine incline alternative.
Free-weight version, deep stretch.
Jeremy's top chest isolation - constant tension the whole range. Cables at chest height; don't clap, press biceps into your sternum and cross slightly.
Lying between cables, stretch to squeeze.
Sweep up-and-in for the upper chest.
Jeremy's #1 for side-delt width. Cable from behind the body loads the delt in a deep stretch where it's strongest. Lead with the elbow.
Lean away slightly, raise to shoulder height.
Constant tension band version.
Targets the upper-chest line. Back flat to the pad, drive elbows together, 1s squeeze.
30 deg bench between cables.
Up-and-in upper-chest sweep.
Jeremy's #1 triceps move - loads the long head (biggest part of the arm) in a deep overhead stretch.
DB/sled overhead extension.
Barbell behind head, long-head stretch.
Pull to the lower-chest line, drive elbows back, squeeze the mid-back. Slow return for the stretch.
Higher pulley angle.
Plate-loaded thickness builder.
Neutral/close grip = better bicep+lat synergy. Drive elbows to hips, full stretch up top.
Wider for lat width.
Bodyweight version.
Chest on an incline bench; with arms hanging, squeeze the shoulder blades together (retract) - hits mid-traps without the lower back.
Constant-tension trap squeeze.
Heavy trap loading.
3D rear delts. Cross cables, drive elbows out and back, control the return.
Pec-deck reverse, easy to overload.
Bent-over DB rear delt.
Bench locks the elbows - no swing, full squeeze. Control the lowering.
Constant tension preacher.
Fixed path to failure.
Lean forward to shift load to the lower/mid chest; descend to a deep stretch. Add weight when 12 is easy.
Assisted/loaded dip alternative.
Bodyweight fallback, full stretch.
Builds the brachialis (pushes the biceps up) + forearms. Neutral grip, no swing.
Constant tension hammer.
Across the body, extra brachialis.
Elbows pinned, spread the rope at the bottom, hard lockout.
Bar handle variation.
Standard cable pushdown.
Finisher - add partial reps at the end to force failure for max side-delt width.
Loaded-stretch cable lateral.
Band constant tension.

meta.type: Jeremy Ethier - upper only, no legs
meta.best: His top picks, leg day swapped for an arms/delts pump
